description: Unique identifier for each session event.
Anonymised user identifier shared across all sessions. Enables trajectory feature computation in the feature store.
Unique session identifier. One row per session.
Content title. Foreign key to content_catalogue.json. Used as content_ref in Layer 7 signal packets.
Behavioral segment. Stable across all sessions for a user. Ground truth for Layer 3 cluster validation.
Session ordinal for this user (1=first, N=most recent). Enables engagement arc trajectory features.

Behavioral event type. session_start=begins watching; progress=mid-session; completion=finished; churn=30+ day inactivity follows; reactivation=return after 30+ day gap. churn_flag and reactivation_flag are mutually exclusive within the same session.
Session timestamp. Exponentially biased toward recent dates within window.
Day within observation window (1=first day). Used for recency weighting in feature store.
Fraction of content watched. Beta-distributed per segment. Forced 0.0 for geo_block unavailability sessions. Primary input to base_completion_probability in Layer 4 twin models.

SIMULATED CONTENT-LEVEL PRIOR, not a per-session observation. Represents the expected fraction of runtime lost to early abandonment for this content item based on segment drop behaviour — generated from Beta distributions calibrated per segment. Should not be interpreted as 'this user dropped X% in this session.' For the per-session binary drop indicator, use early_drop_flag. Triangle constraint: early + mid <= (1 - completion_pct). 0.0 for geo_block sessions.

SIMULATED CONTENT-LEVEL PRIOR, not a per-session observation. Represents the expected fraction of runtime lost to mid-session abandonment for this content item based on segment drop behaviour — generated from Beta distributions calibrated per segment. Should not be interpreted as 'this user dropped X% mid-session.' For the per-session binary drop indicator, use mid_drop_flag. Always >= early_drop_rate. 0.0 for geo_block sessions.

NEW v5.0 REDEFINED. Per-session behavioral fact: 1 if this specific session ended before 25% of content was consumed (completion_pct < 0.25). Derived purely from this session's completion_pct — NOT a threshold on early_drop_rate (which is a content-level aggregate). Mutually exclusive with mid_drop_flag. Always 0 for geo_block sessions (bounce events, not content drops). A churn event almost always triggers this flag given forced completion_pct < 0.40.
range_or_values: 0 | 1 (1 when completion_pct < 0.25; always 0 for geo_block sessions)

description: NEW v5.0 REDEFINED. Per-session behavioral fact: 1 if this specific session ended between 25%–75% of content (0.25 <= completion_pct < 0.75). User got past the opening but did not finish. Mutually exclusive with early_drop_flag. Both flags = 0 when completion_pct >= 0.75 (user substantially finished).
range_or_values: 0 | 1 (1 when 0.25 <= completion_pct < 0.75; mutually exclusive with early_drop_flag)

description: Episodes or content items consumed in session. Log-normal per segment, capped by content type: series/documentary ≤ 8, film ≤ 3, sport ≤ 3, shortform ≤ 20. Watching 16–20 titles in one sitting is implausible for long-form content. 1 for geo_block sessions.

Total session duration in minutes. Bounded by episode_duration_mins × session_depth from content_catalogue — completion of more than the available runtime is impossible. Exception: geo_block sessions are 2–5 minutes regardless of content length (bounce event).

Days since previous session. NULL for session_number=1 — the first observed session in the window has no prior session to gap from. Populating this field for first sessions with a sampled value was semantically wrong (it is not a gap to a known prior event). Sessions 2+ use the real timestamp difference. Approximately 16% of rows are NULL (one per user). reactivation events have gap_days forced to 30–90 when not the first session.
range_or_values: 1 to 90 for sessions 2+ | NULL for session_number=1

description: Content format from content_catalogue.json.
Genre from content_catalogue.json. Used to compute fav_genre and sports_dependency_score in user_profiles.
Episode or film runtime from content_catalogue.json (episode_duration_mins field). Used to bound session_duration_mins. NULL only if not defined in catalogue.
NEW v4.0. 1 if content is a live event (sport matches, live broadcasts). From content_catalogue.json. Live events have near-binary completion (watched or missed), no rewatch.
NEW v4.0. 1 if content is platform-exclusive. From content_catalogue.json. Exclusives show higher completion for binge_heavy and completion_obsessed segments.
NEW v4.0. 1 if content belongs to an existing franchise. From content_catalogue.json. Franchise content has higher baseline engagement from existing fans.

NEW v5.0. Derived engagement lifecycle position for episodic content. Derived from session_number relative to total_episodes in the content catalogue. premiere: session_number=1 (first engagement — platform onboarding or title discovery). mid_season: standard ongoing engagement. penultimate_arc: session_number >= 75% of total_episodes (approaching end — high completion signal). finale: session_number >= total_episodes (end of arc — strong rewatch / churn / reactivation signal). NULL for film, shortform, sport — not episodic, premiere/finale concept does not apply. NOTE: session_number is the user's platform-wide ordinal, not their per-title episode count. Interpretation: user engagement arc position, not literal episode number.
range_or_values: premiere | mid_season | penultimate_arc | finale | NULL (film/shortform/sport ~25% of sessions)

description: True if user churned within 30 days of session. Always True for churn event_type. Mutually exclusive with reactivation_flag — a session cannot be both churn and reactivation simultaneously.

True if session is a return after 30+ day gap. Always True for reactivation event_type. Mutually exclusive with churn_flag. NEW v5.0: only set True if user has at least one prior churn_flag=True session in their history — reactivation is undefined without a prior churn event.
range_or_values: True | False (True only after prior churn in user history)

description: How user found this content. Segment-weighted: binge_heavy skews home/recommendation; quick_churn skews search/external.
Device used for this session. 70% adherence to segment preference per session.

Time-of-day bucket derived from timestamp hour. All timestamps are UTC. time_of_day reflects the UTC hour of the session, not the user's local time. In multi-geo datasets this means a ZA user (UTC+2) watching at 20:00 local is correctly labelled 'evening', but an IN user (UTC+5:30) watching at 17:30 local generates a 12:00 UTC timestamp and is labelled 'afternoon'. Consumers performing local-time analysis should apply geo_country-based UTC offset correction.
range_or_values: morning(05–12h UTC) | afternoon(12–17h UTC) | evening(17–22h UTC) | night(22–05h UTC)

description: 1 if session on Saturday or Sunday.
1 if session coincides with a live event window (is_live_event=True on weekends or high-probability live window). Elevated completion expectations.
1 if user skipped episode intro. binge_heavy: 70%. Others: 30%. ONLY valid for series and documentary content types — always 0 for film, shortform, sport (no intro exists).
1 if session is a rewatch. completion_obsessed: 40%. Others: 10%. Strong content affinity signal.

1 if session used casting (Chromecast/AirPlay — sending content FROM device TO a screen). ALWAYS 0 when device_type=smart_tv — smart_tv is the destination screen, not the source. desktop and laptop are valid casting sources (same capability as mobile/tablet via browser or AirPlay). ~12% probability for all non-smart_tv device types.

NEW v4.0 MOVED HERE. Session-level network connection type (variable per session). 70% adherence to user's primary network, 30% variation. Contextualises avg_bitrate_mbps and avg_network_jitter. Previously was a static user-profile field — moved to session level because network context varies per session (mobile user switches home WiFi to 4G commute).
Buffering interruptions per session. Poisson(1.2). Correlated with avg_network_jitter and peak_hour_congestion_flag.
Average streaming bitrate in Mbps. Correlated with network_type and peak_hour_congestion_flag. Fiber normal: 5–8 Mbps. 4G congested: 1–3.5 Mbps. Diagnostically meaningful — not random.

Network jitter in milliseconds. Correlated with network_type + peak_hour_congestion. Fiber normal: 2–12ms. 4G congested: 25–80ms. Values above 50ms represent service-failure-threshold conditions (ISP outage/severe congestion) rather than normal variation — see network_stress_flag for a binary indicator of these events.
1 during peak congestion (17–22h weekdays, 35% probability). Modulates bitrate and jitter ranges.

NEW v5.0. 1 if avg_network_jitter > 50ms for this session. Derived binary indicator separating service-failure-threshold conditions (ISP outage, severe congestion) from normal network variation. 50ms is a well-established streaming QoE failure threshold — above this, visible buffering and playback interruption are near-certain. ~2.5% of sessions in emerging markets. Consumers should not mix stress and non-stress sessions in the same network quality analysis.
range_or_values: 0 | 1 (~2.5% of sessions on congested 4G in emerging markets)

description: 1 if content was unavailable during session — either geo-blocked or not available due to other reasons. Two sub-cases: geo_block (forced completion=0, depth=1, duration=2–5 min) vs other unavailability (watch data may still be present). See unavailable_reason.

Reason for content unavailability. NULL when content_unavailable_flag=0. geo_block: user's geo_country is not in the title's geo_availability list — session is a bounce (completion=0, depth=1, duration=2–5 min). content_pulled: title was removed from platform. search_no_result: user searched for content not in catalogue.
range_or_values: geo_block | content_pulled | search_no_result | NULL (~95% of sessions)

description: In-app star rating [1.0, 5.0]. SPARSE — NULL for ~70% of sessions. Coverage conditioned on BOTH event_type and completion_pct. completion events: ~45% coverage (user finished — most likely to rate). churn events: ~15% (exit rating, skews negative). reactivation: ~20% (returned user). progress/session_start: base segment rate. Segment-calibrated value. NULL=user did not rate. 0 is not a valid rating.
